User Story: As a planner, I want the app to open to a clear start screen when no saved roadmap exists, so I can begin intentionally.
Expected Behavior: When local storage has no valid roadmap, the start screen is visible, Resume is hidden, Start from a blank outline is primary, and privacy text is visible.
Latest Test Result: No saved data shows the start screen, hides Resume, and shows the local-only privacy note.

User Story: As a returning planner, I want the app to reopen my last saved work automatically, so I do not lose progress.
Expected Behavior: When valid autosave exists, init deserializes it, enters the studio, and shows a restore toast with Start fresh.
Latest Test Result: Saved state auto-restores into studio and shows restore toast.

Feature: Resume last session from start screen
User Story: As a returning planner on the start screen, I want to resume the last session, so I can continue where I left off.
Expected Behavior: When autosave exists and the user is on the start screen, Resume last session appears with relative save time and loads the saved roadmap.
Latest Test Result: Resume last session reloads saved roadmap data.

User Story: As a planner, I want to start from a blank outline, so I can build a new roadmap from scratch.
Expected Behavior: Clicking Start from a blank outline creates one pillar, one workstream, no initiatives, file name Untitled roadmap, and opens the Structure tab.
Latest Test Result: Blank start creates one pillar/workstream and opens Structure with tabs ordered Structure, Initiatives, Roadmap.

Feature: Return to start screen from brand
User Story: As a planner, I want the Roadmap Studio brand control to return me to the start screen without deleting my session, so I can choose a different starting action.
Expected Behavior: Clicking Roadmap Studio in the toolbar renders the start screen while keeping autosaved session data available.
Latest Test Result: Brand returns to start screen while leaving the saved session resumable.

User Story: As a planner, I want to save a project file, so I can reopen it later on another machine.
Expected Behavior: Clicking Save downloads a JSON project file using the current file name or roadmap fallback and serialized state.
Latest Test Result: Save downloads a JSON project file with serialized roadmap state.

User Story: As a planner, I want to open a Roadmap Studio project file, so I can continue a saved roadmap.
Expected Behavior: Selecting a valid project JSON deserializes structure, items, dates, value type, value, sequence, file name, and fiscal year, then enters the studio.
Latest Test Result: Opening a valid project JSON restores the roadmap.

User Story: As a planner, I want invalid project files rejected, so bad data does not corrupt my roadmap.
Expected Behavior: Selecting an invalid or wrong-shaped JSON shows the invalid Roadmap Studio project file error on the start screen.
Latest Test Result: Invalid project JSON is rejected with a visible error.

User Story: As a planner, I want to switch between Structure, Initiatives, and Roadmap in the expected order, so the workflow matches how data is entered.
Expected Behavior: Toolbar tabs appear as Structure, Initiatives, Roadmap. Clicking each tab marks it active and displays only that stage.
Latest Test Result: Blank start creates one pillar/workstream and opens Structure with tabs ordered Structure, Initiatives, Roadmap.

User Story: As a planner, I want to set the fiscal year start month, so roadmap quarters and imported quarter labels match my organization.
Expected Behavior: The fiscal year selector lists all months, defaults to July, updates S.fyStart, rerenders the roadmap, and affects fiscal year/quarter calculations.
Latest Test Result: Fiscal year selector updates the selected month and rerenders quarter/FY labels.

User Story: As an editor, I want undo and redo controls, so I can recover from changes safely.
Expected Behavior: Undo is disabled with no history, becomes enabled after tracked edits, restores the previous snapshot, and redo reapplies it.
Latest Test Result: Toolbar undo and redo restore and reapply a pillar rename.

User Story: As an editor, I want keyboard shortcuts for undo and redo, so editing feels standard.
Expected Behavior: Cmd/Ctrl+Z runs undo and Shift+Cmd/Ctrl+Z runs redo.
Latest Test Result: Keyboard undo and redo work on editable structure changes.

User Story: As a planner, I want summary counts for the outline, so I can check scope quickly.
Expected Behavior: Structure metadata shows pillar and workstream counts with correct singular/plural wording.
Latest Test Result: Blank start creates one pillar/workstream and opens Structure with tabs ordered Structure, Initiatives, Roadmap.

User Story: As a planner, I want to rename pillars inline, so the roadmap structure matches my business categories.
Expected Behavior: Changing a pillar input pushes undo, updates the pillar name, and rerenders dependent views.
Latest Test Result: Pillar name edits persist after change events.

User Story: As a planner, I want to add pillars, so I can create additional strategic categories.
Expected Behavior: Clicking Add pillar appends a blank pillar with one New workstream, rerenders, focuses and selects the new pillar name.
Latest Test Result: Add pillar appends a pillar and focuses the new name input.

User Story: As a planner, I want to reorder pillars, so the roadmap appears in the right executive order.
Expected Behavior: Pillar up/down buttons swap adjacent pillars, are disabled at boundaries, and preserve initiatives assigned to those pillars.
Latest Test Result: Pillar up/down controls reorder adjacent pillars.
Retest Result: Passed after adding data-p to pillar move/delete controls.
Notes: Initial full run found pillar move controls lacked the pillar id needed by the shared structure action handler.

User Story: As a planner, I want to delete a pillar, so outdated categories and their initiatives are removed.
Expected Behavior: Deleting a pillar removes its workstreams and initiatives; if no pillars remain, the app creates a fallback New pillar and New workstream.
Latest Test Result: Deleting a pillar removes it and leaves a valid remaining outline.
Retest Result: Passed after adding data-p to pillar move/delete controls.
Notes: Initial full run found pillar delete controls lacked the pillar id needed by the shared structure action handler.

User Story: As a planner, I want to rename workstreams inline, so initiative lanes match team language.
Expected Behavior: Changing a workstream input pushes undo, updates the workstream name, and rerenders dependent views.
Latest Test Result: Workstream name edits persist after change events.

User Story: As a planner, I want to add a workstream to a pillar, so I can expand the roadmap structure.
Expected Behavior: Clicking Add workstream appends a blank workstream to that pillar, rerenders, and focuses the new workstream input.
Latest Test Result: Add workstream appends a focused blank workstream.

User Story: As a keyboard user, I want Enter in a workstream to add another below it, so outline entry is fast.
Expected Behavior: Pressing Enter in a workstream commits the current value, inserts a blank workstream immediately below, rerenders, and focuses it.
Latest Test Result: Enter in a workstream adds a new workstream below and focuses it.

User Story: As a keyboard user, I want Backspace on an empty workstream to remove it, so cleanup is fast.
Expected Behavior: Pressing Backspace in an empty workstream removes it when the pillar has more than one workstream, deletes initiatives in that workstream, rerenders, and moves focus to the previous workstream.
Latest Test Result: Backspace on an empty workstream removes it when another workstream remains.

User Story: As a planner, I want to reorder workstreams inside a pillar, so lanes are presented in the desired order.
Expected Behavior: Workstream up/down buttons swap adjacent workstreams and are disabled at boundaries.
Latest Test Result: Workstream up/down controls reorder adjacent workstreams.

User Story: As a planner, I want to delete a workstream, so outdated lanes and their initiatives are removed.
Expected Behavior: Deleting a workstream removes its initiatives; if the pillar would have no workstreams, the app adds a New workstream fallback.
Latest Test Result: Deleting a workstream removes it and leaves a valid fallback lane when needed.

User Story: As a planner, I want each workstream to show its initiative count, so I can see which lanes have work.
Expected Behavior: Workstream rows show empty tick/no count when no initiatives exist and a filled tick plus singular/plural item count when initiatives are assigned.
Latest Test Result: Structure workstreams show initiative counts for populated lanes.

User Story: As a planner, I want a clear empty state, so I know how to start adding initiatives.
Expected Behavior: When there are no initiatives, the Initiatives tab shows the empty note and an Add initiative button.
Latest Test Result: Initiatives tab shows empty state and Add initiative.

User Story: As a planner, I want to add an initiative, so I can enter dated work under a workstream.
Expected Behavior: Add initiative creates a row assigned to the first pillar and first workstream, named New initiative, starting today, ending 90 days later, Savings, blank value, Not Started, and focuses the name input.
Latest Test Result: Add initiative creates the expected default row and values.

User Story: As a planner, I want to rename initiatives inline, so each bar has the correct work name.
Expected Behavior: Changing the initiative name pushes undo, updates S.items, and rerenders the grid and roadmap label.
Latest Test Result: Initiative name edit persisted.

User Story: As a planner, I want to move an initiative to another pillar, so ownership structure can change.
Expected Behavior: Changing Pillar updates pillarId and automatically sets wsId to the first workstream in the selected pillar.
Latest Test Result: Changing pillar reassigns the initiative to the first workstream in that pillar.

User Story: As a planner, I want to assign an initiative to a workstream, so it appears in the correct roadmap lane.
Expected Behavior: Changing Workstream updates wsId and rerenders the grid and roadmap lane.
Latest Test Result: Changing workstream updates the selected workstream.

User Story: As a planner, I want to edit initiative dates, so the timeline reflects real timing.
Expected Behavior: Start and End date inputs parse to UTC dates; if end becomes before start, the changed date clamps the other date to preserve a non-negative range.
Latest Test Result: Date edits clamp start/end to prevent negative duration.

User Story: As a planner, I want to mark one-day milestones, so gates render differently from duration work.
Expected Behavior: Checking Milestone sets end equal to start, disables/fades the End input, and renders the roadmap item as a diamond.
Latest Test Result: Milestone toggle sets a one-day item and disables End.

User Story: As a planner, I want to track initiative status, so roadmap visuals show health.
Expected Behavior: Status supports Not Started, On Track, At Risk, Complete. At Risk adds a risk border, Complete fades the item, and Not Started renders black on the roadmap.
Latest Test Result: Status selection persisted.

User Story: As a planner, I want to classify each initiative as Savings or Avoidance, so roadmap color and totals communicate impact type.
Expected Behavior: Savings/Avoidance select normalizes to one of two values. Savings uses green and Avoidance uses blue in roadmap bars, milestones, totals, and tooltips.
Latest Test Result: Savings/Avoidance selection persisted.

User Story: As a planner, I want to enter a dollar value for each savings or avoidance initiative, so financial impact appears on the roadmap.
Expected Behavior: The value input accepts numeric currency-like text, stores parsed money, formats as USD in the grid, shortens values on roadmap labels and totals, and includes full money in tooltip.
Latest Test Result: Dollar value parses and formats as USD.

User Story: As a planner, I want to enter the percentage of each initiative's value that has been realized, so realized savings can be tracked from the same grid.
Expected Behavior: The Initiatives grid includes a % Realized column after $ Value. The field accepts percentage-like input, stores a clamped 0-100 percentage, formats it with a percent sign, and persists through save/open/autosave.
Latest Test Result: Realized percentage accepts fractional entry, stores percent points, and formats with a percent sign.

User Story: As a planner, I want owner values remembered as suggestions, so repeated ownership entry is faster.
Expected Behavior: Owner input uses a datalist populated from unique non-empty owner values; changing an owner refreshes suggestions.
Latest Test Result: Owner entry refreshes datalist suggestions.

User Story: As a planner, I want to reorder initiative rows, so the grid can match my preferred sequence.
Expected Behavior: Row up/down controls swap adjacent initiatives and preserve row data.
Latest Test Result: Initiative row up/down control reorders rows.

User Story: As a planner, I want to delete an initiative, so removed work disappears from the grid and roadmap.
Expected Behavior: Row delete removes the initiative from S.items and rerenders the grid, metadata, totals, and roadmap.
Latest Test Result: Initiative row delete removes the row.

User Story: As a planner, I want a useful roadmap message when no structure exists, so I know what to do next.
Expected Behavior: If structure is empty, the timeline area displays Add a pillar in the Structure tab to begin.
Latest Test Result: Empty structure timeline fallback message renders.

User Story: As a planner, I want counts for all initiatives and dated initiatives, so I know whether items are ready for the timeline.
Expected Behavior: Roadmap metadata shows total initiative count and count of initiatives with start dates.
Latest Test Result: Roadmap renders counts, grouped realized financial totals, per-pillar totals, fiscal/quarter layout, today marker, lanes, item labels, and money labels.

User Story: As a planner, I want savings and avoidance totals above the roadmap, so I can see total financial impact by type.
Expected Behavior: Roadmap legend totals sum all numeric item values by normalized Savings or Avoidance type and show short currency values.
Latest Test Result: Roadmap renders counts, grouped realized financial totals, per-pillar totals, fiscal/quarter layout, today marker, lanes, item labels, and money labels.

User Story: As a planner, I want realized savings shown under the Savings total, so I can compare planned savings against savings already realized without cluttering the header.
Expected Behavior: The roadmap header shows Realized under the Savings total. It sums Savings initiatives only as $ Value multiplied by % Realized, ignores Avoidance for this metric, and shows the result as short currency.
Latest Test Result: Realized financial totals sum their own value types precisely and ignore the other value type.

User Story: As a planner, I want realized avoidance shown under the Avoidance total, so I can compare planned avoidance against avoidance already realized without cluttering the header.
Expected Behavior: The roadmap header shows Realized under the Avoidance total. It sums Avoidance initiatives only as $ Value multiplied by % Realized, ignores Savings for this metric, and shows the result as short currency.
Latest Test Result: Realized financial totals sum their own value types precisely and ignore the other value type.

User Story: As a presenter, I want each expanded roadmap pillar to show its own Savings, Avoidance, and Realized totals, so I can showcase one pillar at a time with its financial impact visible.
Expected Behavior: Each expanded roadmap pillar header shows Savings, Avoidance, and Realized totals for initiatives inside that pillar only. Collapsed pillars keep the title visible but hide the per-pillar totals.
Latest Test Result: Roadmap renders counts, grouped realized financial totals, per-pillar totals, fiscal/quarter layout, today marker, lanes, item labels, and money labels.

User Story: As a planner, I want the roadmap to render pillars, workstreams, quarters, fiscal years, and bars, so the plan is presentation-ready.
Expected Behavior: Roadmap SVG renders FY bands, quarter labels, pillar bands, workstream labels, rows, bars, milestones, and today marker using current structure, dates, fiscal year start, status, and value type.
Latest Test Result: Roadmap renders counts, grouped realized financial totals, per-pillar totals, fiscal/quarter layout, today marker, lanes, item labels, and money labels.

User Story: As a planner, I want initiative bars to show dollar amounts when present, so the impact is visible without hovering.
Expected Behavior: Roadmap labels include item name only when value is zero/blank and item name plus short money when value is non-zero.
Latest Test Result: Roadmap renders counts, grouped realized financial totals, per-pillar totals, fiscal/quarter layout, today marker, lanes, item labels, and money labels.

User Story: As a planner, I want hover details on roadmap items, so I can inspect exact metadata without returning to the grid.
Expected Behavior: Hovering a bar or milestone shows name, pillar/workstream, date or range, Savings/Avoidance with full money, status, and owner.
Latest Test Result: Roadmap tooltip shows item metadata, full money, status, and owner.

User Story: As a planner, I want to drag a roadmap item, so I can adjust timing visually.
Expected Behavior: Dragging a bar or milestone horizontally moves start and end together by rounded day offset, pushes undo on pointer up, and does nothing in presentation mode.
Latest Test Result: Dragging a roadmap item moves its dates.

User Story: As a planner, I want to drag start and end edges, so I can adjust initiative duration visually.
Expected Behavior: Dragging the start edge changes start without passing end; dragging the end edge changes end without passing start; pointer up records the change for undo.
Latest Test Result: Dragging an end edge changes the item end date.

User Story: As a viewer, I want the roadmap to rerender on resize, so the timeline remains usable at different viewport widths.
Expected Behavior: When the window resizes in Roadmap or presentation view, renderTimeline runs and recalculates timeline width and scale.
Latest Test Result: Roadmap rerenders with different SVG width after viewport resize.

User Story: As a presenter, I want to collapse or expand each roadmap pillar section, so I can showcase one pillar at a time.
Expected Behavior: Each roadmap pillar band has a plus/minus toggle. Clicking it hides or shows that pillar's workstream lanes and initiatives while keeping the pillar header visible, and the collapsed state persists in saved/autosaved state.
Latest Test Result: Roadmap pillar bands collapse, persist collapsed state, and expand back to show hidden lanes.

User Story: As a presenter, I want Not Started initiatives to render black on the roadmap, so they are visually distinct from Savings and Avoidance items.
Expected Behavior: Roadmap items with status Not Started render with black fill and white text instead of green/blue value-type fill with only an outline. The roadmap legend includes Not Started.
Latest Test Result: Not Started roadmap items render black and the legend includes Not Started.

User Story: As a planner, I want to export the current roadmap as a PNG, so I can paste it into decks or messages.
Expected Behavior: When a roadmap SVG exists, PNG creates a 2x canvas snapshot and downloads roadmap.png.
Latest Test Result: PNG export downloads a non-empty roadmap.png.

User Story: As a planner, I want a clear message if PNG export is unavailable, so I know to open Roadmap first.
Expected Behavior: If no timeline SVG exists, clicking PNG alerts Open the Roadmap tab first.
Latest Test Result: PNG outside Roadmap shows the expected alert.

User Story: As a presenter, I want to export the roadmap to PowerPoint, so each pillar can be reviewed as its own readable slide.
Expected Behavior: PowerPoint export downloads a .pptx deck with one slide per pillar. Each slide includes the pillar name, Savings, Avoidance, Realized totals, fiscal year labels, quarter markers, workstream labels, and initiative bars or milestones for that pillar only.
Latest Test Result: PowerPoint export downloads a deck with one human-readable slide per pillar, including fiscal years.

User Story: As a presenter, I want a clean full-screen roadmap view, so I can present without editing chrome.
Expected Behavior: Clicking Present sets presenting true, adds presentation styling, switches to Roadmap if needed, requests fullscreen, hides toolbar and stage header, and fits the SVG.
Latest Test Result: Present mode hides editing chrome and shows presentation state.

User Story: As a presenter, I want a dark/light toggle, so the roadmap works in different presentation environments.
Expected Behavior: Dark toggles S.dark, the body dark class, the Dark/Light button label, and rerenders with the presentation dark palette.
Latest Test Result: Dark presentation toggle changes palette state and button label.

User Story: As a presenter, I want to exit presentation cleanly, so I can return to editing.
Expected Behavior: Exit button, Escape, or leaving fullscreen clears presenting/dark classes, exits fullscreen when needed, resets transform/width, and rerenders the timeline.
Latest Test Result: Exit presentation clears presentation state.

User Story: As a planner, I want changes saved locally, so accidental refreshes do not lose my roadmap.
Expected Behavior: renderAll schedules autosave, autosave writes serialized state to localStorage key roadmapStudio.v1 after 400 ms, and loadAutosave returns only valid saved structures.
Latest Test Result: Autosave writes changed roadmap state to localStorage after edits.

User Story: As a planner, I want to intentionally start fresh after auto-restore, so restored work does not trap me in the previous session.
Expected Behavior: The restore toast includes Start fresh; clicking it hides the toast and renders the start screen while leaving saved data available.
Latest Test Result: Start fresh from restore toast returns to start while keeping saved data available.

User Story: As a planner with sensitive data, I want clear local-only messaging, so I know the app does not upload my roadmap.
Expected Behavior: The start screen states Everything stays in this browser. Nothing is uploaded anywhere.
Latest Test Result: Local-only privacy statement is present.

User Story: As a planner with sensitive data, I want the app to avoid remote runtime scripts, so entered roadmap data is not exposed to CDN-hosted code.
Expected Behavior: The app has no remote script URLs, no ExcelJS/CDN dependency, no Excel import/export controls, and loads the PowerPoint writer from the local vendor folder.
Latest Test Result: External runtime URLs and Excel controls are absent; the local PowerPoint bundle loads.

User Story: As a planner with sensitive data, I want browser-enforced network egress blocking, so the app cannot make fetch/XHR/WebSocket/beacon-style calls.
Expected Behavior: The app declares a meta Content-Security-Policy with default-src 'none', connect-src 'none', form-action 'none', object-src 'none', and no external origins.
Latest Test Result: Meta CSP blocks connection APIs and external resource origins for the single-file app.

User Story: As a keyboard or assistive-tech user, I want controls with labels and visible focus, so the app is operable beyond pointer use.
Expected Behavior: The app has focus-visible styling, tablist/tab roles, aria labels on grid inputs, undo/redo buttons, and roadmap SVG role img with aria-label.
Latest Test Result: Basic labels, roles, and visible focus styling are present.

User Story: As a motion-sensitive user, I want animations disabled when reduced motion is requested, so the app respects OS preferences.
Expected Behavior: When prefers-reduced-motion is reduce, all transitions and animations are disabled by CSS.
Latest Test Result: Reduced-motion CSS guard is present.
